name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 13.04.2026

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 13.04.2026.pdf

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 06.04.2026
texts.text: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 06.04.2026

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:
texts.text: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on April 10, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 64.10 lakh ha as compared to 63.33 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 30.59 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.
texts.text:  As on April 10, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 64.10 lakh ha as compared to 63.33 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 30.59 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on 27.03.2026, 32.26% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over India. Approximately 67% area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Gram  (66%),  Urdbean  (86%)  &  Moong  (63%);  71.56%  area  of  Oilseeds majorly in Rapeseed & Mustard (75.53%); 38.31% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (54.47%), 25.57% area of Rice and 6.00% area of Wheat  has been harvested for Rabi crops 2025-26.
texts.text:  As on 27.03.2026, 32.26% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over India. Approximately 67% area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Gram  (66%),  Urdbean  (86%)  &  Moong  (63%);  71.56%  area  of  Oilseeds majorly in Rapeseed & Mustard (75.53%); 38.31% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (54.47%), 25.57% area of Rice and 6.00% area of Wheat  has been harvested for Rabi crops 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update:
texts.text: 2. Weather Update:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Temperature: Maximum temperature is likely to be above normal in Tamil Nadu, many parts of Andhra Pradesh, many parts of Odisha, Chhattisgarh and coastal regions of Karnataka.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum temperature is likely to be above normal in Tamil Nadu, many parts of Andhra Pradesh, many parts of Odisha, Chhattisgarh and coastal regions of Karnataka.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  April  12  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  22% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 40%; (ii) Northwest India: 3%; (iii) Central India: 51%; and South Peninsula: 11%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  April  12  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  22% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 40%; (ii) Northwest India: 3%; (iii) Central India: 51%; and South Peninsula: 11%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: o A fresh  Western  Disturbance  is  likely  to  affect  Western  Himalayan  Region  from  15 th April, 2026.
texts.text: o A fresh  Western  Disturbance  is  likely  to  affect  Western  Himalayan  Region  from  15 th April, 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: o Scattered rainfall with thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds is expected across many parts of  Northwest,  Northeast,  East,  and  South  Peninsular  India  between  9-13  April,  with  stronger winds reaching 50-60 kmph in some eastern regions.
texts.text: o Scattered rainfall with thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds is expected across many parts of  Northwest,  Northeast,  East,  and  South  Peninsular  India  between  9-13  April,  with  stronger winds reaching 50-60 kmph in some eastern regions.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: o Heavy rainfall is likely at isolated places over Arunachal Pradesh, South Assam, Tripura, and Sikkim, while hailstorm activity may occur over Assam & Meghalaya on 9 April.
texts.text: o Heavy rainfall is likely at isolated places over Arunachal Pradesh, South Assam, Tripura, and Sikkim, while hailstorm activity may occur over Assam & Meghalaya on 9 April.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: o Light  to  moderate  rain  with  thunderstorms  will  also  affect  parts  of  Tamil  Nadu,  Kerala, Rayalaseema, and South Interior Karnataka during this period.
texts.text: o Light  to  moderate  rain  with  thunderstorms  will  also  affect  parts  of  Tamil  Nadu,  Kerala, Rayalaseema, and South Interior Karnataka during this period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: o Gradual rise in maximum temperatures likely over Northwest, Central & East India by 3-6°C during next 4-5 days. As a result, maximum temperatures are likely to be above normal over these region after 2 days.
texts.text: o Gradual rise in maximum temperatures likely over Northwest, Central & East India by 3-6°C during next 4-5 days. As a result, maximum temperatures are likely to be above normal over these region after 2 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: o Heat wave conditions very likely in isolated pockets of Saurashtra  & Kutch on 13th; Odisha during 14th -16th; Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, Chhattisgarh during 16th -18th; Marathawada on 16th April.
texts.text: o Heat wave conditions very likely in isolated pockets of Saurashtra  & Kutch on 13th; Odisha during 14th -16th; Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, Chhattisgarh during 16th -18th; Marathawada on 16th April.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status:  As  on  10  April  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 82.070 billion cubic meter, which is 44.71% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 117.66 percent of last  year's  storage  and  127.01  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status:  As  on  10  April  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 82.070 billion cubic meter, which is 44.71% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 117.66 percent of last  year's  storage  and  127.01  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last

texts.label: text
texts.orig: year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Groundwater  Levels:  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text:  Groundwater  Levels:  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.
texts.text:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.
texts.text:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.
texts.text:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:
texts.text: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (02 April - 08 April 2026), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 76%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 08 April 2026) is higher than LPA by 27%.
texts.text:  During the week (02 April - 08 April 2026), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 76%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 08 April 2026) is higher than LPA by 27%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, thunderstorm activity is likely to continue over the northeast & adjoining east India during the week.
texts.text: Overall, thunderstorm activity is likely to continue over the northeast & adjoining east India during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, rainfall is likely to be below normal over most parts of the country.
texts.text: Overall, rainfall is likely to be below normal over most parts of the country.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Details of each Segment
texts.text: Details of each Segment

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX
texts.text: 2. TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 1 (10 April - 16 April 2026):
texts.text: Week 1 (10 April - 16 April 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall,  Day  temperatures  are  likely  to  remain  below  normal  to  near  normal  over  most  part  of northwest, central & east India during next 2-3 days and gradually become above normal thereafter.
texts.text:  Overall,  Day  temperatures  are  likely  to  remain  below  normal  to  near  normal  over  most  part  of northwest, central & east India during next 2-3 days and gradually become above normal thereafter.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (10 April - 16 April 2026):
texts.text: Week 2 (10 April - 16 April 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Low to moderate Probability of Heat Wave conditions likely during the week 2 over some parts of Odisha, Jharkhand, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, & north Coastal Andhra Pradesh. Appreciably above normal (+3.1 to +5.0°C) maximum temperatures are likely over some parts of Odisha, north Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Jharkhand, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Gangetic West Bengal and East Uttar Pradesh; normal to above-normal  maximum  temperatures  ((+1.6  to  +3.0°C)  are  likely  over  most  parts  of  Northwest India,  and  many  parts  of  the  East  &  Northeast,  South  and  Western  parts  of  India.  Below-normal weekly maximum temperatures are likely in isolated places over the rest of the country.
texts.text:  Low to moderate Probability of Heat Wave conditions likely during the week 2 over some parts of Odisha, Jharkhand, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, & north Coastal Andhra Pradesh. Appreciably above normal (+3.1 to +5.0°C) maximum temperatures are likely over some parts of Odisha, north Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Jharkhand, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Gangetic West Bengal and East Uttar Pradesh; normal to above-normal  maximum  temperatures  ((+1.6  to  +3.0°C)  are  likely  over  most  parts  of  Northwest India,  and  many  parts  of  the  East  &  Northeast,  South  and  Western  parts  of  India.  Below-normal weekly maximum temperatures are likely in isolated places over the rest of the country.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No warm night conditions are likely during the week.
texts.text:  No warm night conditions are likely during the week.

texts.orig:  Rice : About 30.59 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.30 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Rice : About 30.59 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.30 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Pulses :  About 11.54 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 10.71 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Pulses :  About 11.54 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 10.71 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item

texts.orig:  Soil moisture during 02 April - 08 April 2026 is similar/better than the average of the past 10 years in major parts of Rajasthan, Gujarat, Maharashtra,  Telangana,  A.P., Karnataka, Kerala,  Madhya Pradesh, Uttar  Pradesh,  Punjab,  Haryana,  Uttarakhand,  H.P.,  J  &  K,  Bihar,  Jharkhand,  West  Bengal  and  N.E. States.

States.
